title: Myrcia maguirei
taxonomy: Myrcia maguirei is classified within the family Myrtaceae, commonly known as the myrtle family. This family consists of approximately 3,000 to 6,000 species, with many being evergreen shrubs or small trees. Myrcia maguirei is a member of the genus Myrcia, which comprises around 800 species, primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Myrcia maguirei is further supported by its morphological and anatomical characteristics, such as its fruit and leaf structure. Its classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships within the family Myrtaceae and the genus Myrcia.
uses: Myrcia maguirei is a plant species that has been studied for its potential medicinal uses, but specific information on its uses is limited.
